title: Situational Awareness
summary: Projects that superintelligence is achievable by 2027-2028 through scaling current approaches, triggering an intelligence explosion and intense geopolitical competition.
social: Mass societal disruption as AI surpasses human cognition across all domains; potential for extreme social stratification between those who control AI and those who don't.
technological: Assumes continued exponential scaling of compute and algorithmic efficiency; predicts AGI-level systems within 2-3 years followed by rapid recursive self-improvement.
economic: Economic concentration in whoever achieves superintelligence first; massive value capture by a tiny number of firms; potential for post-scarcity or total economic disruption.
environmental: Massive energy demands for superintelligent systems; environmental impact depends entirely on whether AI is directed toward sustainability or unconstrained growth.
political: Frames as a geopolitical race primarily between US and China; argues for government-lab partnerships and potential nationalization of AI development.

title: The Whirlwind: AI's Rapid Transformation
institution: Tony Blair Institute for Global Change
summary: Argues AI will transform governance, public services and the economy within 5 years, requiring immediate institutional adaptation rather than wait-and-see approaches.
social: Public service transformation creates winners and losers; digital divide deepens unless proactively managed; healthcare and education radically reshaped.
technological: Assumes rapid capability gains in language models enabling automation of most knowledge work; multimodal AI reshaping service delivery.
economic: GDP growth accelerates but labor share of income falls; massive retraining needs; potential fiscal windfall from productivity gains if managed well.
environmental: Relatively optimistic on environmental impact through AI-optimized systems, but acknowledges energy costs of AI infrastructure.
political: Calls for proactive government transformation; new institutions needed; risk of democratic erosion if governments can't keep pace with corporate AI deployment.

title: Machines of Loving Grace
summary: Optimistic scenario for powerful AI: if safety challenges are solved, AI could dramatically accelerate progress in health, poverty reduction, governance, and science within 5-10 years.
social: Dramatic improvements in global health and poverty; but benefits depend heavily on equitable access and governance structures.
technological: Assumes powerful AI that is reliably aligned and controllable; human-AI collaboration as primary mode rather than full automation.
economic: Broadly positive economic transformation with AI as amplifier of human capability; acknowledges need for redistribution mechanisms.
environmental: Significant environmental benefits through AI-accelerated clean energy, materials science, and climate modeling.
political: Optimistic about AI supporting better governance, but contingent on solving alignment and maintaining democratic oversight.

title: The Compendium: AI & Economic Transformation
summary: Comprehensive economic analysis of transformative AI: models GDP growth trajectories, labor displacement timelines, and capital concentration under various automation scenarios.
social: Social disruption concentrated in knowledge-work sectors first; potential for mass purposelessness even with economic safety nets.
technological: Detailed modeling of AI capability trajectories across task types; estimates timeline to full automation of various job categories.
economic: Projects potential 10-30% GDP growth but with severe distributional problems; models show capital owners capturing majority of gains without intervention.
environmental: Notes massive compute energy requirements; models environmental impact of AI infrastructure buildout.
political: Emphasizes need for novel economic policy; existing institutions ill-equipped for speed of transformation.

title: AI 2027: Near-Term Trajectories
summary: Detailed scenario modeling the path from current AI to transformative systems by 2027, including lab dynamics, capability jumps, and societal response timelines.
social: Rapid capability gains outpace societal adaptation; potential for crisis moments when AI capabilities visibly surpass human performance on economically important tasks.
technological: Models specific capability milestones: autonomous research by 2026, broadly superhuman reasoning by 2027; assumes continued scaling plus algorithmic breakthroughs.
economic: Economic transformation begins in earnest by 2026 with autonomous AI agents performing professional services; labor market disruption accelerates rapidly.
environmental: Energy infrastructure becomes critical bottleneck; AI companies become largest energy consumers.
political: Government response lags private sector by 12-18 months; regulatory frameworks designed for current AI become obsolete.

title: White Collar Wipeout
summary: Scenario in which AI rapidly automates professional and knowledge work, creating mass unemployment among previously secure middle-class workers while GDP continues to grow.
social: Middle class social contract breaks down; professional identity crisis; mental health epidemic among displaced knowledge workers.
technological: Assumes AI reaches and exceeds professional-grade performance in law, finance, consulting, medicine, software development within 3-5 years.
economic: GDP grows 5-15% annually but labor share of income collapses; Engels Pause 2.0 where workers become poorer even as economy grows; consumption crisis follows.
environmental: Modest direct environmental impact; potential positive impact from AI-optimized systems offset by growth in compute infrastructure.
political: Fiscal crisis as income tax base erodes; demand for UBI/safety nets surges; political polarization between beneficiaries and losers of AI transition.

title: The Precipice: Existential Risk from AI
summary: Frames advanced AI as the most significant existential risk humanity faces this century, with a 10% chance of existential catastrophe from unaligned superintelligence.
social: Existential risk framing implies all social structures at risk; emphasizes intergenerational ethics and responsibility to future generations.
technological: Focuses on alignment problem: sufficiently advanced AI systems may pursue goals misaligned with human values in ways that are catastrophic and irreversible.
economic: Economic considerations secondary to survival; but notes that competitive dynamics create race conditions that increase risk.
environmental: Environmental concerns subsumed under existential risk framing.
political: Argues for unprecedented global coordination; likens to nuclear weapons governance but with harder verification problems.

title: The Coming Wave
summary: Argues AI and synthetic biology represent an unstoppable wave of technology that will fundamentally reshape power structures; calls for 'containment' as the central challenge.
social: Technology diffusion means transformative capabilities become widely accessible, including to malicious actors; societal resilience is the key challenge.
technological: Frames AI as part of a broader wave including synthetic biology; emphasizes the containment problem — keeping powerful tech under control while allowing benefits.
economic: Economic disruption seen as inevitable; focus is on whether institutions can adapt fast enough to manage the transition without catastrophic failures.
environmental: Highlights synthetic biology intersection; environmental risks from both AI energy use and biotech applications.
political: Central argument is about governance: the nation-state may not be the right unit to govern these technologies; calls for new institutional forms.

title: AI and the Economy: Macro Impacts
summary: IMF analysis projecting AI will affect 40% of global jobs, with advanced economies most exposed; warns of significant inequality increases without policy intervention.
social: Advanced economies face highest exposure; developing countries may be shielded initially but miss out on productivity gains; global inequality dynamics shift.
technological: Conservative technological assumptions relative to other scenarios; focuses on current and near-term AI capabilities rather than speculative superintelligence.
economic: Projects 40% job exposure in advanced economies; models fiscal impacts including tax base erosion and social spending increases; emphasizes macro-prudential approach.
environmental: Limited environmental analysis; focuses on economic and fiscal dimensions.
political: Emphasizes multilateral coordination; calls for updating social safety nets, education systems, and tax frameworks across G20 nations.

title: AI and National Security Transformation
summary: Analysis of how AI transforms national security landscape: autonomous weapons, cyber capabilities, intelligence analysis, and strategic stability between great powers.
social: Military AI deployment creates new ethical dilemmas; autonomous weapons raise accountability questions; civilian-military AI divide grows.
technological: Focuses on military-relevant AI capabilities: autonomous systems, cyber operations, intelligence processing, decision support for strategic command.
economic: Defense spending restructured around AI; new arms race dynamics; economic impact through military-industrial complex transformation.
environmental: Environmental impact of military AI infrastructure; potential for AI-optimized environmental monitoring but also for AI-enabled resource extraction.
political: Strategic stability between nuclear powers disrupted; AI-enabled first-strike capabilities change deterrence calculus; calls for new arms control frameworks.
